scenario: Married filing jointly with two W-2 incomes ($42,000 + $28,000 = $70,000). Taxpayer made $7,000 traditional IRA contribution (not covered by employer plan — fully deductible). Saver's Credit at 10% rate (MFJ AGI $63,000 in $50,001–$76,500 range). Credit = 10% × $2,000 = $200.
source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, illustrative case based on IRA deduction (Pub 590-A, Worksheet 1-2) and Saver's Credit (Form 8880) modules; IRC §219, §25B; Rev. Proc. 2024-40 IRA_CONTRIBUTION_LIMIT_2025 = $7,000; SAVERS_CREDIT_AGI_MFJ_2025 rate10 = $76,500
